title: Chest Pain with Imaging
text: 65-year-old male with a 2-hour history of crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm, associated with diaphoresis and mild dyspnea. Past medical history significant for hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and a 30 pack-year smoking history. Current medications: lisinopril 10 mg daily, atorvastatin 40 mg nightly. Vital signs: BP 158/94 mmHg, HR 102 bpm, RR 18/min, SpO2 97% on room air. 12-lead ECG shows 2 mm ST elevation in leads V2-V4. Chest X-ray obtained. Please evaluate for acute coronary syndrome and assess imaging findings.

title: Lung Mass with Medication Review
text: 58-year-old female referred for evaluation of an incidental 3.2 cm spiculated right upper lobe lung mass found on CT chest performed for unrelated reasons. Patient has a 40 pack-year smoking history and quit 5 years ago. She reports a 6 kg unintentional weight loss over the past 3 months and intermittent hemoptysis. Current medications include metformin 1000 mg twice daily for type 2 diabetes, omeprazole 20 mg daily, and ibuprofen 400 mg as needed. PET-CT shows FDG-avid right upper lobe mass with ipsilateral hilar lymphadenopathy (SUVmax 12.4). Please evaluate the imaging, provide oncological staging and treatment recommendations, and review medications for interactions with potential chemotherapy regimens.

title: Multi-Specialty Complex Case
text: 72-year-old male with a complex medical history presenting for multidisciplinary evaluation. History includes: (1) Coronary artery disease with prior CABG 8 years ago, currently on aspirin 81 mg, clopidogrel 75 mg, metoprolol succinate 50 mg, and rosuvastatin 20 mg. (2) New diagnosis of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) confirmed on axillary lymph node biopsy 2 weeks ago. (3) Stage IIIA disease on PET-CT with mediastinal and bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, and a 4.5 cm anterior mediastinal mass causing mild tracheal deviation. (4) Echocardiogram shows EF 45% with regional wall motion abnormalities. (5) eGFR 52 mL/min/1.73m². (6) Current medications also include furosemide 40 mg daily and spironolactone 25 mg daily for heart failure with reduced ejection failure. Patient is being considered for R-CHOP chemotherapy. Please provide a comprehensive multi-specialty care plan addressing cardiac risk during chemotherapy, imaging surveillance strategy, medication management including potential drug interactions with R-CHOP, and oncological treatment recommendations.
